text.title: Interview Insight Tagging Schema Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Interview Insight Tagging Schema application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Schema pins six tag categories: Job, Pain, Workaround, Outcome, Segment, Channel. Other categories are rejected unless added by a versioned schema change.
rule.rationale: Open categories drift; six fixed axes survive multiple research quarters.

rule.statement: Each category has a closed value list (≤25 values). Free-tag is rejected; new values require a versioned schema change.
rule.rationale: Open tag space produces 200+ near-duplicate tags within a year.

rule.statement: Each insight card carries ≥1 tag from each category. 'No segment' / 'no job' is forbidden; explicit 'cross-segment' / 'unknown-job' values cover unknowns.
rule.rationale: Missing tags break cross-interview joins downstream.

rule.statement: Schema carries a semver version + last_reviewed date. Tag additions are PRs against the canonical schema, never local forks.
rule.rationale: Local forks turn the team back into tag chaos within one quarter.

rule.statement: Segment values are sourced from the operator's segment map; ad-hoc segment tags are rejected.
rule.rationale: Free-form segments break downstream join with billing / behavioural data.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
